mnemonics.title: NODE STRUCTURE MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: DATA + NEXT = NODE. Each node has DATA (the cargo) and NEXT (the address of the next truck). Think of a TRAIN: each car (node) carries cargo (data) and coupler to next car (next pointer).

mnemonics.title: INSERTION AT BEGINNING
mnemonics.content: NEW POINTS TO HEAD, THEN HEAD POINTS TO NEW. Like adding a new person at the front of a line: they become the new leader, and they point to who was first.

mnemonics.title: LINKED LIST TRAVERSAL
mnemonics.content: FOLLOW THE LINKS UNTIL YOU HIT NULL. Like reading a chain of letters: each letter tells you where the next one is, until you reach the end marked 'STOP'.

acronyms.expansion: Linked List - A linear data structure with nodes containing data and pointers
acronyms.usage: Used to implement dynamic data storage where size changes during runtime

acronyms.expansion: First In First Out - A queue principle
acronyms.usage: Linked lists can implement queues efficiently for顺序 processing

acronyms.expansion: Last In First Out - A stack principle
acronyms.usage: Linked lists can implement stacks efficiently for reverse-order processing

acronyms.expansion: Not a Value - A special pointer value marking end of list
acronyms.usage: Used as sentinel to indicate no more nodes exist in the linked list

visualTips.visualization: DRAW A BOX SPLIT INTO TWO: left half = DATA field, right half = POINTER field with arrow pointing right. The arrow shows where the next node lives.

visualTips.visualize: Imagine arrows forming a chain from box to box, with the final arrow pointing to a 'NULL' sign or empty box.
Draw a new box, draw arrow from new box to existing first box, then move 'HEAD' label to point to the new box.
Use three arrows: one pointing back (previous), one current, one forward (next). Flip each arrow direction as you move through.

analogies.analogy: Think of a TREASURE HUNT: each clue points to the next location. You start at the first clue (head) and follow directions until there are no more clues (NULL). Unlike a map (array), you cannot jump directly to clue 5.
Like a HOTEL WITH NO FIXED ROOMS: guests can check in or out anytime, rooms don't have to be consecutive. Array is like numbered seats in a theater - fixed and consecutive.
Think of a POINTER AS A STREET ADDRESS: it tells you where to go next, but isn't the actual destination. Each house (node) has the address of the next house written on it.
Like a DEAD END SIGN: it tells you the road ends here, there's nothing more beyond. It's a clear signal to stop following the chain.
Like REMOVING A CAR FROM A TRAIN: you unhook it from the car behind it and connect that car directly to the one in front. The removed car is no longer part of the train.
